summary: I reviewed the design-coverage of the Plan-phase technical design against the Define-phase business requirements. I read the design-coverage-critic `input.json` (no resolved inputs, so I located artifacts directly), then read `requirements.md` (R1–R13), `design.md`, and the codebase `discovery_notes.md` to verify claims. I built a requirement-by-requirement coverage matrix confirming every one of R1–R13 maps to a concrete design element (inline init script, Stimulus `theme_controller`, `theme` cookie precedence, matchMedia change listener, CSS semantic-token layer, token sweep across the ~23 styled views, and `StatusHelper` dark variants), and checked for over-design — finding none (the two beyond-strict elements, the `ThemeHelper` SSR reader and the ui-style-guide update, are a justified robustness measure and a mandated project convention respectively). I emitted a **pass** verdict with no findings to `verdict.json`, recording one non-blocking advisory note: the design should remove the R10 `matchMedia` listener inside `toggle()` to fully close a narrow same-page-session R11 window — a correctness refinement for the Implementer, not a coverage gap.
